title: Progressions
learning_outcomes: Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Arithmetic Progressions (AP) sums and term relations, Geometric Progressions (GP) ratios and infinite sums, Harmonic Progressions (HP) relations.

content_sections.title: Progressions
content_sections.content:
### Arithmetic, Totients & Combinatorics
Quantitative Aptitude focuses on number relationships, algebraic systems, and probability calculations.

#### Euler's Totient Theorem
For coprimes $a$ and $n$, the remainder of $a^{\phi(n)} \pmod n$ is $1$, where $\phi(n)$ is Euler's totient (count of numbers coprime to $n$ up to $n$):
$$\phi(n) = n \prod_{p \mid n} \left(1 - \frac{1}{p}\right)$$
For primes, $\phi(p) = p - 1$, yielding Fermat's Little Theorem: $a^{p-1} \equiv 1 \pmod p$.

#### Product Consistency Constraint
If $A \times B = C$ and $C$ remains constant, a fractional increase in $A$ by $x/y$ requires a fractional decrease in $B$ by $\frac{x}{x + y}$ to maintain product consistency.

#### Stars-and-Bars Partitions
To distribute $n$ identical objects among $k$ distinct bins:
*   *Empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n + k - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.
*   *No empty bins allowed:* $\binom{n - 1}{k - 1}$ partitions.

### Depth Analysis: Progressions
In production configurations, we analyze how Arithmetic Progressions (AP) sums and term relations, Geometric Progressions (GP) ratios and infinite sums, Harmonic Progressions (HP) relations affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Progressions
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Progressions is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Progressions
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Progressions loops.
